name: pa_always_zero
code:
proof fn phi_1_pa_always_zero(v: usize)
    requires
        v != 0usize,
    ensures
        spec_usize2pa(v) == 0usize,
{
}
reason: If PA extraction collapses all nonzero values to 0, alloc_and_map_io always returns a null physical address for mapped IO pages.

name: mem_valid_vacuous
reason: If MEM_valid is trivially true for every address, usize2pa's postcondition provides no real constraint on physical pages returned by alloc_and_map_io.

name: zero_entry_present
reason: If zero-valued entries decode as present, uninitialized L2 slots spuriously satisfy alloc_and_map_io's L2-resolution precondition, enabling mapping into garbage page tables.

name: perm_always_user
code:
proof fn phi_4_perm_always_user(v: usize)
    requires
        MEM_valid(spec_usize2pa(v)),
    ensures
        spec_usize2page_entry_perm(v).user == true,
{
}
reason: If every page-entry permission has the user bit set, IO pages allocated by alloc_and_map_io are accessible from unprivileged code, breaking device-isolation guarantees.

name: pa_non_injective
code:
proof fn phi_5_pa_non_injective(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        a != b,
        spec_usize2page_entry(a).perm.present,
        spec_usize2page_entry(b).perm.present,
    ensures
        spec_usize2pa(a) == spec_usize2pa(b),
{
}
reason: If distinct present page-table entries always decode to the same physical address, alloc_and_map_io could alias a new IO mapping onto an already-used physical frame.
